1.7: Contact Head Office Mortgage Department for cases where borrower and mortgagor differ.
1.14: The firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: The firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
4.5b: Send the certificate of title to the Head Office Mortgage Department.
4.1: Obtain a copy of the valuation report from the applicant.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports must be submitted if they indicate a negative impact on security or are required by the mortgage offer.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if compliant with the Search Code monitored by the PCCB.
The search agency must have adequate Professional Indemnity Insurance.
Must satisfy Part 1 requirements and provide an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted subject to Part 1 requirements and provision of an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3b: Original planning permission should be kept with the Title Deeds.
Provide a copy to the Society only if specified in the offer conditions.
5.5.3c: Original building regulation consents should be kept with the Title Deeds.
Provide a copy to the Society only if specified in the offer conditions.
5.5.3d: Original certificates of lawful use or development should be kept with the Title Deeds.
Provide a copy to the Society only if specified in the offer conditions.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable up to 20% of the property area.
Refer cases exceeding 20% to Head Office Mortgage Department.
Compliance with Part 1 paragraph 5.7.2 is mandatory.
5.7.1c: Refer flying freehold matters to the contact in 1.11a.
5.8.1: Accepts buildings converted into up to four flats if the borrower occupies one.
Security must encompass the leasehold flat.
If the borrower owns the freehold, the security must include the freehold title.
5.8.5: Accepts cross-over freehold reversions between two leasehold flats.
Security must cover the borrower's leasehold flat and the freehold interest in the neighboring flat.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent or insolvent landlords is accepted if it resolves the issue and allows for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.1: The lease must have at least 50 years remaining at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted if an unqualified Certificate of Title can be given and Section 9 requirements are met.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charge receipts do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.14.13: Notice of service evidence does not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.17.5a: Certified copies of power of attorney are acceptable; originals are not required.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.20.1: Report lease terms for solar PV panels that fail to meet UK Finance minimum requirements to the contact in 1.11a.
5.20.3: Leases for solar PV panels must meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
Leases must include a mortgage break clause exercisable on possession.
Panel removal and damage repair costs must be the provider's responsibility.
The building insurer must be notified of the installation.
5.20.4: Details of any existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.
6.4.4: Report all incentives immediately to the Head Office Mortgage Department.
Do not submit the Disclosure of Incentives Form unless specifically requested.
6.6.3: Counterpart or certified copy tenancy agreements do not need to be sent.
6.6.4: Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) are not accepted.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes: NHBC, Checkmate's Castle 10, HAPM, Premier Guarantee.
6.7.2: Place guarantee documentation with Title Deeds.
Send a copy to the Society only if requested in offer conditions.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes do not need to be sent.
6.7.4: The Society will proceed without a new home warranty scheme.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates must be submitted.
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the borrower.
First self-build instalment is issued to the solicitor; subsequent instalments go to the borrower.
7.3: Consent to mortgage is required from all occupants aged 17 or older.
8.1: Firms are not permitted to advise specified third parties.
9.1: Place the original indemnity insurance policy with Title Deeds and send a copy to the Society.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover the mortgage amount plus 25%.
10.2b: A minimum of 1 day notice is required for the advance.
10.3: There are no standard deductions from the mortgage advance.
10.7: If completion is delayed, return funds electronically to the Society's bank account immediately.
10.10: Advances may be held for a maximum of 2 working days before being returned.
10.11: The Society may charge for interest lost due to delayed return of funds.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds and title documents do not need to be sent.
Original deeds should be sent to HM Land Registry or retained on file if using e-registration/certified copies.
14.2.1: Other documents should be sent to: Head Office, SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
14.2.2: Submit all documents specified in Part 1 and any additional documents required by the mortgage offer.
16.1.1: Contact for title documents: Head Office, SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: 'The Common Seal of Scottish Building Society was hereto affixed in the presence of .................. duly authorised by the Board of Directors.'
16.3.1: There is no standard form for transfers or deeds of covenant.
16.3.4: The transfer of equity must be submitted.
16.4.2: Proposed tenancy agreements do not need to be sent.
16.5.3a: Send deeds of variation to SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
16.5.3b: Send deeds of rectification to SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
16.5.3c: Send deeds of easement to SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
16.5.3d: Send option agreements to SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
17.2.1a: Send repayment remittances to the office administering the mortgage account.
Send discharges to Head Office, SBS House, 193 Dalry Road, Edinburgh EH11 2EF.
